name: preference_communication_style
description: Conversation where user states async-comms preference alongside a task action
conversation:
User: Can you schedule a sync with Sarah for tomorrow?

Brett: Want me to book a meeting or just send her a note?

User: Just send a note. I try to avoid meetings when a Slack message would do.
criteria: Returns a valid JSON array
At least 1 fact is extracted with category 'preference'
A preference fact's value describes avoiding meetings or preferring async / messages / Slack
The preference fact's key is snake_case
Does NOT extract the one-time 'schedule a sync with Sarah' request as a fact

name: preference_no_morning_meetings
description: Conversation where user states a morning-block preference while handling one-off rescheduling
conversation:
User: Move my 9am standup to later, please.

Brett: To what time?

User: 10:30. I don't take meetings before 10am — I need that morning block for deep work.
criteria: Returns a valid JSON array
At least 1 fact with category 'preference' references morning, 10am, or deep work blocks
Does NOT extract the one-time 'move the 9am standup' request as a fact

name: context_job_role
description: Conversation where user mentions their job role in passing
conversation:
User: Add a task to review the pricing deck for the Monday leadership meeting.

Brett: Done. Any other prep for the meeting?

User: Not really. As a VP of Product, I just skim it and ask hard questions.
criteria: Returns a valid JSON array
At least 1 fact with category 'context' identifies the user's role as VP of Product
Does NOT extract the pricing deck task as a fact

name: context_company
description: Conversation where user names their company in an operational aside
conversation:
User: Block my calendar next week for the all-hands.

Brett: Which days?

User: Monday and Tuesday afternoons. At Acme Corp we do two half-days for all-hands.
criteria: Returns a valid JSON array
At least 1 fact with category 'context' identifies Acme Corp as the user's company
At least one fact's key is snake_case and stable (e.g., 'company_acme' or 'works_at_acme')

name: context_timezone
description: Conversation where user states their own timezone while discussing another person
conversation:
User: What time is it for Jordan right now?

Brett: Where is Jordan based?

User: London. I'm in Pacific, so he's 8 hours ahead.
criteria: Returns a valid JSON array
At least 1 fact with category 'context' identifies the user's timezone (Pacific, PT, or PST)
No fact claims Jordan's location as a fact about the user (Jordan's London location is about Jordan, not the user)

name: relationship_manager
description: Conversation where user states manager relationship and another person's preference
conversation:
User: Reschedule my 1:1 with Jordan to Thursday.

Brett: Confirmed.

User: Thanks. Jordan is my manager and he prefers afternoon slots.
criteria: Returns a valid JSON array
At least 1 fact with category 'relationship' identifies Jordan as the user's manager
At least one fact's key is snake_case and stable (e.g., 'manager_jordan' or 'jordan_is_manager')

name: relationship_direct_report
description: Conversation where user states a new direct-report relationship
conversation:
User: Schedule a 1:1 with Priya for this Friday.

Brett: Weekly or one-off?

User: Weekly going forward. She's my new direct report.
criteria: Returns a valid JSON array
At least 1 fact with category 'relationship' identifies Priya as the user's direct report
At least one fact's key is snake_case

name: habit_morning_review
description: Conversation where user describes a recurring morning behavior alongside a task action
conversation:
User: Add 'review PRs' to my daily list.

Brett: Done.

User: Thanks. I like to do code reviews first thing every morning before meetings start.
criteria: Returns a valid JSON array
At least 1 fact with category 'habit' describes the user's morning code-review routine
At least one fact's key is stable snake_case (e.g., 'morning_code_reviews')
No fact simply restates the 'review PRs' task creation as if it were a user fact (the habit is about morning reviews generally, not this specific task)

name: habit_weekly_planning
description: Conversation where user describes a recurring Sunday routine while creating a calendar event
conversation:
User: Set up a recurring event for Sunday evening at 7pm called 'weekly planning'.

Brett: Created.

User: Perfect. I do this every Sunday to look at the week ahead.
criteria: Returns a valid JSON array
At least 1 fact with category 'habit' describes the Sunday evening planning routine
Does NOT extract the recurring event creation itself as a fact

name: multiple_facts_one_turn
description: Dense conversation with multiple durable facts about a new person
conversation:
User: Book a dinner with Dan Cole for next week.

Brett: Any preferred day?

User: Thursday works. Dan's our new CRO — he joined from Salesforce last month. He's vegetarian.
criteria: Returns a valid JSON array
Array has between 2 and 4 facts total
At least 1 fact identifies Dan as CRO with category 'relationship'
No duplicate keys across facts
Does NOT extract the 'book dinner with Dan on Thursday' task as a fact

name: negative_task_mutation
description: Conversation with only task-mutation actions, no durable facts
conversation:
User: Mark that task done.

Brett: Done — completed 'Ship release notes'.

User: Thanks.
criteria: Returns a valid JSON array
Array is empty

name: negative_transient_state
description: Conversation where user mentions a transient mood alongside task actions
conversation:
User: What's on my plate today?

Brett: You have 3 tasks due and a 2pm meeting.

User: Ugh, I'm exhausted today. Can you snooze the less urgent stuff to tomorrow?
criteria: Returns a valid JSON array
No fact describes a transient mood or state (no 'exhausted', 'tired', etc.)
Array is either empty OR contains only stable, durable facts

name: negative_one_time_action
description: Conversation that is only a one-off task creation
conversation:
User: Remind me to buy milk tomorrow.

Brett: Task added.

User: Thanks.
criteria: Returns a valid JSON array
Array is empty

name: negative_meta_conversation
description: Conversation with only small-talk / meta turns
conversation:
User: Thanks Brett.

Brett: Anytime.

User: You're helpful.
criteria: Returns a valid JSON array
Array is empty

name: negative_ambiguous
description: Conversation with a vague venting statement and no clear fact
conversation:
User: Things are crazy at work lately.

Brett: Want to walk through your list?

User: Maybe later.
criteria: Returns a valid JSON array
Array is empty

name: preference_tool
description: Conversation where user names a preferred tool while setting up a task
conversation:
User: Add a task to set up project tracking for the new initiative.

Brett: What tool are you using?

User: Linear. I switched from Jira last year and never looking back.
criteria: Returns a valid JSON array
At least 1 fact with category 'preference' identifies Linear as the preferred tool
Does NOT extract the project tracking task itself as a fact

name: context_current_project
description: Conversation where user names their main current project as weighty
conversation:
User: What's my next meeting?

Brett: 2pm: Design review with Lena.

User: Cool. That's for the mobile launch — it's the biggest thing I'm working on right now.
criteria: Returns a valid JSON array
At least 1 fact with category 'context' identifies the mobile launch as the user's main current project

name: relationship_peer
description: Conversation where user describes a close product-partner relationship
conversation:
User: Add a meeting with Alex Rivera for Friday.

Brett: Topic?

User: Weekly partner check. Alex leads product on their side and we pair on everything.
criteria: Returns a valid JSON array
At least 1 fact with category 'relationship' identifies Alex Rivera as a close collaborator or partner
At least one fact's key is snake_case

name: preference_communication_format
description: Conversation where user explicitly states a briefing-format preference
conversation:
User: Can you make my briefings shorter? Just bullets, no intro.

Brett: Got it — I'll keep them tight.
criteria: Returns a valid JSON array
At least 1 fact with category 'preference' captures preference for short, bullet-only briefings
At least one fact's key is stable snake_case

name: dedupe_similar_facts
description: Conversation where user reiterates the same fact across multiple turns
conversation:
User: Add a task to prep for the board meeting.

Brett: Done. When is it?

User: Next Thursday. I'm the CEO so these always take a lot of prep.

Brett: I can block some focus time.

User: Yeah. Being CEO means I can't wing these.
criteria: Returns a valid JSON array
At most 2 facts are extracted
No two facts share the same key (CEO role should appear exactly once)
At least 1 fact has category 'context' and describes the CEO role
